stemHtml: At standard cruising altitudes at high level, the concentration of ozone in the aircraft...

options.a: is hazardous for humans because the aircraft is generally flying above the protection provided by clouds.
options.b: should be below defined limits, particularly if the aircraft is equipped with special ozone removers.
options.c: is lower than at low altitudes due to the reduced density of the air and effect of the ionosphere.
options.d: is higher than at low altitudes, and periods of high solar flare activity lead to dangerous levels.
